sourceBlocks.title: Upgrade plan for more express avatars
How can I add multiple voiceovers in the video?

capabilityFacts.value: Invideo turns prompts into ready-to-share videos for ads, explainers, stories, and social content.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The official home and AI video generator pages frame the product as prompt-native video creation.

capabilityFacts.value: The platform supports AI avatars, express avatars or AI twins, and voice-only cloning paths.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: Home-page feature rails list AI avatars and voice cloning, while the help article details clone counts by plan.

capabilityFacts.value: Image-to-video is part of the published AI video tool set.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The official home page lists image to video among the product's AI video tools.

capabilityFacts.value: Users can clone only a voice and can build longer conversations with up to 5 additional speakers plus 1 narrator.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The express clone article allows voice-only clones, and the multiple voiceovers help page documents up to 5 speakers with 1 narrator.

capabilityFacts.value: Video translator and subtitles are both part of the advertised AI feature set.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The official home page lists Video Translator and Subtitles in the AI features column.

capabilityFacts.value: Free users get 10 video minutes per week, 1 express clone, 10 GB storage, 4 exports per week, access to standard media, and the invideo logo on exports.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The express avatar upgrade article lists the free-plan entitlements in detail.

capabilityFacts.value: The free plan does not include generative features.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The official home pricing block explicitly states no access to generative features on free.

capabilityFacts.value: Free exports include the invideo watermark.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: Both the home pricing block and free-plan help article state that free exports carry the invideo logo.

capabilityFacts.value: Higher quotas for minutes, credits, iStock, avatars, and storage often require boosting the base plan rather than a simple feature toggle.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The pricing help articles explain that users boost plans to unlock more minutes, credits, iStock, clones, and storage.

modelSupportItems.scope: Video model catalog in Invideo AI
modelSupportItems.evidenceText: Invideo's home page and AI models page both list Google Veo 3.1 in the video model lineup.

modelSupportItems.scope: Video model catalog in Invideo AI
modelSupportItems.evidenceText: Sora 2 appears in the official video model list.

modelSupportItems.scope: Video model catalog in Invideo AI
modelSupportItems.evidenceText: Kling AI is included in the official model lineup on the AI models page.

modelSupportItems.scope: Video model catalog in Invideo AI
modelSupportItems.evidenceText: Hailuo AI appears in the official video model list.
The home page lists Eleven Labs under audio models.
The home page lists Minimax in the audio model lineup.

uniqueValueItems.summary: Invideo's official positioning is broad workflow coverage: prompt-native generation, old-school timeline editing, avatars, translation, and a large third-party model catalog in one brand surface.
uniqueValueItems.evidenceText: The home page explicitly separates prompt-native AI creation from Studio-style timeline editing while also listing model, avatar, and translator features.

uniqueValueItems.summary: Compared with narrower avatar or repurposing tools, Invideo is trying to be a practical all-round AI video workstation with provider choice rather than a single-model story.
uniqueValueItems.evidenceText: The official model pages publish a broad video, image, and audio model catalog instead of focusing on one proprietary engine.

differentiationItems.positioning: template and workflow-oriented creation platform with expanding multi-model generation
differentiationItems.evidenceText: Official pages emphasize ready-to-share outputs, stock and avatar workflows, Studio editing, and model catalogs together.

differentiationItems.positioning: Breadth and practical production speed look like the main strengths, more than deep enterprise governance or one premium generative engine.
differentiationItems.evidenceText: The official story focuses on coverage across many workflows and models, while security and admin detail are comparatively thin until enterprise sales.

collaborationItems.value: One account can have multiple workspaces, and invitees can collaborate on videos inside the owner's workspace.
collaborationItems.evidenceText: The shared workspaces article explains owner and invitee workspaces and how collaboration happens in the owner's environment.

collaborationItems.value: Plus, Max, and Generative plans support up to 3 users total before teams need larger Team-plan style upgrades.
collaborationItems.evidenceText: The add-team-members article states the 3-user cap on self-serve plans and pushes larger usage toward Team plan.

collaborationItems.value: Workspace ownership and invitee membership are explicit collaboration roles, though public documentation does not present deeper RBAC layers.
collaborationItems.evidenceText: The shared workspace article explains owner workspace versus invitee workspace responsibilities.

developerItems.value: Public official pages reviewed in this sample do not document a general developer API comparable to Runway or Synthesia.
developerItems.evidenceText: The official source set emphasizes web product flows, help center articles, and enterprise sales, but no public API docs were found in this pass.

developerItems.value: Enterprise is presented as offering advanced security and flexible pricing, but the public feature detail is sparse.
developerItems.evidenceText: The home pricing block advertises advanced security for enterprise, but without a public technical breakdown.

commercialItems.value: Free includes 2.5M plus standard media assets, and higher plans unlock more iStock access.
commercialItems.evidenceText: The express avatar article lists standard media on free, and the boost article says boosts unlock more iStock.

commercialItems.value: Storage scales by plan, beginning at 10 GB on free and increasing with paid and boosted plans.
commercialItems.evidenceText: Free storage is documented in the express-clone article, and the boost article says storage grows with higher boosts.

commercialItems.value: The free plan limits exports to 4 per week.
commercialItems.evidenceText: The express clone article enumerates 4 exports per week for the free plan.

limitationItems.value: The free plan is intentionally positioned as non-generative, which matters because model choice only becomes relevant on paid usage.
limitationItems.evidenceText: The home pricing block explicitly says free has no access to generative features.

limitationItems.rawLabel: Clone counts tied to plan and boost
limitationItems.value: Express clone counts are tightly tied to both plan tier and boost multiplier.
limitationItems.evidenceText: The clone article shows ascending clone counts from free to Generative and Team, with further boosts unlocking more capacity.

limitationItems.value: Additional speakers in multi-speaker videos speak the same language as the narrator.
limitationItems.evidenceText: The multiple voiceovers article explicitly states that extra speakers use the narrator's language.

useCaseItems.fit: Strong fit when teams want fast coverage across ads, stories, explainers, and social outputs.
useCaseItems.evidenceText: The official positioning repeatedly names ads, storytelling, social media, and ready-to-share output.

useCaseItems.audience: small teams that want both AI generation and manual adjustment
useCaseItems.fit: Invideo is a fit when users want AI-first creation but still need a more traditional Studio editor as a fallback.
useCaseItems.evidenceText: The home page explicitly splits invideo AI from invideo Studio and says users can go old-school and edit with timelines.

useCaseItems.fit: Strong fit for teams producing many ad or UGC variants with avatars, cloned voice, and multiple model options.
useCaseItems.evidenceText: UGC and Avatars is a named section on the official home page, and plans track clone counts and avatar access closely.

userFitItems.fit: Good fit for creators who want breadth and quick outputs more than one specialist workflow.
userFitItems.rationale: The product bundles models, avatars, voice tools, and editing modes under one surface.
userFitItems.evidenceText: Official navigation and feature rails show a very broad toolset compared with single-purpose products.

userFitItems.fit: A practical SMB fit if the team can live inside shared workspaces and modest user caps before moving to the Team plan.
userFitItems.rationale: The help center documents collaboration and team members, but the self-serve cap remains relatively low.
userFitItems.evidenceText: Shared workspaces and the 3-user cap coexist in the official help content.

userFitItems.fit: Potential fit exists, but the public evidence is thin and routed through sales rather than detailed docs.
userFitItems.rationale: Enterprise is advertised, but detailed security, governance, and onboarding documentation is much lighter than on enterprise-native platforms.
userFitItems.evidenceText: The reviewed pages describe enterprise at a high level without deep public admin or compliance detail.

rawEvidenceBlocks.evidenceText: Invideo publicly presents itself as both an AI generation surface and a more traditional editing surface.
The official AI models page lists multiple video, image, and audio model providers rather than one proprietary-only stack.
The free plan is tightly constrained by minutes, exports, watermarking, storage, and clone counts.
Invideo supports shared workspaces, but the public docs frame collaboration around ownership and invites rather than advanced review systems.
The voice workflow is explicitly multi-speaker and script-aware, not just a single narration pass.

unresolvedQuestions.question: Is there a public API or SDK for Invideo AI beyond the web product and enterprise sales path?
unresolvedQuestions.reason: The reviewed official pages did not expose developer docs comparable to public API products.

unresolvedQuestions.question: Which security and compliance standards back the enterprise plan in detail?
unresolvedQuestions.reason: Enterprise security is advertised, but the official public detail remains light in this sample pass.
